title: Euclidean gcd via well-founded recursion equals Nat.gcd
informal_statement: Define euclidGcd by the Euclidean algorithm (euclidGcd b a = if b = 0 then a else euclidGcd (a % b) b) via genuine well-founded recursion on the first argument (a % b < b, not a direct predecessor of b). Prove euclidGcd b a = Nat.gcd b a for all b, a -- full correctness against Mathlib's canonical gcd, not just a base-case unfolding fact.
informal_proof_idea: euclidGcd is defined via WellFoundedLT.fix with motive (fun _ => Nat -> Nat), threading the second gcd argument through the step function and decreasing on the first argument via Nat.mod_lt. Since WellFoundedLT.fix does not reduce definitionally, a helper lemma euclidGcd_unfold (the one-step unfolding equation) is proved first via `unfold euclidGcd; rw [WellFoundedLT.fix_eq]`. The main correctness theorem then follows by strong induction on b (Nat.strong_induction_on): the zero case is Nat.gcd's own zero-left unfolding; the successor case rewrites via euclidGcd_unfold, applies the induction hypothesis at the strictly smaller a % b, then aligns with Nat.gcd_rec (explicitly instantiated as `Nat.gcd_rec b a` to target the correct occurrence, since the bare lemma name is ambiguous about which side of the goal to rewrite).
formal_statement_pp: theorem euclidGcd_eq_gcd : ∀ (b a : ℕ), euclidGcd b a = Nat.gcd b a

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode a4a2a972, kernel_verified on the first submit_module attempt in that episode). This is a renamed re-verification of the same construction first worked out under the name myGcd in an earlier probing episode (d83a52a3, also kernel_verified, 4 steps) within this same cycle -- discovered afterward that another concurrent agent had already landed a distinct, weaker packet, elementary.induction.mygcd_wellfounded.v1 (only myGcd a 0 = a, via Nat.strongRecOn/Nat.strongRecOn_eq), reusing the name myGcd in this same namespace; renamed to euclidGcd throughout and re-ran the identical (already-debugged) proof recipe end-to-end through the tracked loop to avoid a duplicate top-level Lean identifier across the two packets' standalone modules, rather than silently hand-renaming an already-verified script. Key debugging lesson from the myGcd episode, preserved here: WellFoundedLT.fix does not reduce by rfl; `unfold <name>; rw [WellFoundedLT.fix_eq]` is required (a bare `show ... ; rw [WellFoundedLT.fix_eq]` leaves an unclosed goal because the recursive-call site still refers to the folded definition name, not its raw fix-based unfolding). Strengthens the already-closed 'well-founded (non-structural) recursion via SubmitModule' backlog item in packets/elementary/induction/QUEUE.md from a base-case-only fact to full correctness against Nat.gcd.
